title_en: Models Have a Shelf Life Now
excerpt_zh: Fable 5 上线时说「算在套餐里能用到 6 月 22 号」。我把它当宝贝，六个会话并排猛用，平时一周用不完的额度三天多榨干。结果它没到 22 号就提前下线了——模型不是软件，是生鲜，标签上的日期还是乐观估计。
excerpt_en: Fable 5 showed up promising "on your plan until June 22." I treated it like treasure — six sessions at once — and burned a week's quota in three days. Then it went offline before the 22nd. A model isn't software; it's fresh produce, and the date on the label is the optimistic one.

title_zh: 门槛没有消失，只是搬了家
title_en: The Bar Didn't Drop. It Moved.
excerpt_zh: 过去难在执行：记语法、配环境、读报错。现在难的变成另外三件事——说清楚问题、拆清楚任务、判断结果对不对。同样用 AI，人和人的差距不是缩小了，是被放大了。
excerpt_en: The hard part used to be execution: syntax, environments, errors. Now it's three other things — stating the problem, breaking down the work, judging the result. Same AI, and the gap between people is being amplified, not shrunk.

title_zh: 零基础的「基础」，从来不是代码基础
excerpt_zh: 一个完全不会写代码的设计师，两天做出了团队真在用的内部工具。她只做了三件事：把痛点讲清楚、对每一版说「对」或「不对」、在关键决策点拍板。
excerpt_en: A designer who can't write a line of code shipped a tool her team actually uses — in two days. She did exactly three things: described the pain clearly, said "right" or "wrong" to each iteration, made the calls at decision points.

title_zh: 用户分层，按场景不按人
title_en: Segment by Scenario, Not by Person
excerpt_zh: 同一个人，写代码的时候是极客，处理法务的时候就是小白，写文案的时候又成了要求「指哪打哪」的专家。用户分层从来不该按人分，该按场景分。
excerpt_en: The same person is a geek when coding, a novice when facing legal paperwork, and an expert demanding pixel-level control when writing copy. Segmentation should never cut by person — it cuts by scenario.

excerpt_zh: 提示词就是代码，只不过运行它的不是编译器，是模型。结构清楚的指令，误执行率就是低。你对提示词有多严谨，AI 就对你的需求有多严谨。
excerpt_en: Prompts are code — the runtime just happens to be a model, not a compiler. Well-structured instructions get misexecuted less. The rigor you put into your prompt is the rigor AI puts into your request.

excerpt_zh: 网络会断，上下文会爆，任务会跑偏。把活拆成原子块，断在哪就能从哪续上。系统的好坏，不看顺风时跑多快，看断电后还剩多少。
excerpt_en: Networks drop, context overflows, tasks drift. Cut work into atomic blocks and you resume wherever it broke. Judge a system not by how fast it runs downwind, but by how much survives a power cut.

excerpt_zh: 传统路径是「学好了再做」，结果学员死在第三周的语法课里。现在我让他们第一节课就跑通一个能用的东西。不求一次全懂，求逐步打通。
excerpt_en: The traditional path is "learn properly, then build" — and students die in week three, somewhere in the syntax chapters. Now everyone gets something working in lesson one. Don't aim to understand it all at once; aim to break through, step by step.

title_zh: 语音输入的瓶颈，不在识别
title_en: The Bottleneck in Voice Input Isn't Recognition
excerpt_zh: 识别准确率从 93% 卷到 95%，没人愿意多花那 0.2 秒去润色。但用户要的从来不是「准」，是「我说一段话，出来一段能直接发出去的字」。
excerpt_en: Voice tools grind recognition from 93% to 95%; nobody spends the extra 0.2 seconds on polishing. But what users want has never been "accurate" — it's "I speak, and what comes out is something I can ship."

title_zh: Claude 账号登录问题 · 完整解决方案
excerpt_zh: 学员里十个有八个卡在登录这一步。这篇把所有可能的卡点和对应方案讲清楚。
excerpt_en: Most students get stuck at login. Every common failure point and the matching fix.

title_zh: Claude 登录常见问题 · FAQ
excerpt_zh: 按报错现象查。找到一样的就照着做。
excerpt_en: Symptom-based troubleshooting list for Claude login & Claude Code.

excerpt_zh: 做 Typevoise 之前我以为难点是识别。后来发现真正的瓶颈在另一个地方。
excerpt_en: Before Typevoise I thought the hard part was recognition. The real bottleneck was somewhere else.

title_zh: AI 时代最危险的不是不会用
title_en: The Real Risk in the AI Era Isn't That You Don't Use It
excerpt_zh: 教零基础学员的时候我观察到三种「假装会用 AI」的姿势。哪一种都能让聪明人陷在原地。
excerpt_en: Teaching beginners, I noticed three postures of pretending to use AI. Any one of them can trap a smart person in place.

excerpt_zh: 做课的人总想给每个概念配比喻。但学员的脑子里只有三个槽位。多了不是加分，是互相冲撞。
excerpt_en: Course designers want a metaphor for every concept. But students only have three slots. More than that, metaphors don't add — they collide.

title_zh: 为什么自建一个 Claude Code 中转网关
title_en: Why I Self-Host a Claude Code Gateway
excerpt_zh: 朋友问我为什么不直接订 Claude Pro。一开始我以为是为了省钱，做完之后才明白不是。
excerpt_en: A friend asked why I don't just subscribe to Claude Pro. I thought it was about money. Turns out it wasn't.
